COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Jan-29-2007 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On May-10-11 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly for trial by court.
Appearances:
JAMES E. SELL, Esq. on behalf of plaintiffs LUDMILA ERSHOV and PETER SCHWALBENBERG, individually and as trustees for the SCHWALBENBERG FAMILY REVOCABLE TRUST
JEFFREY A. CHADIC, Esq. on behalf of defendant JOHN B. WONG, individually and dba BOXER GROUP

Matter assigned from Dept. 206 to put terms of the settlement after parties were sent to Judge Pro Tem. Terms of the settlement are as follows: Within 30 days from this date of Jan. 29, 2007, defendant JOHN B. WONG, individually and dba BOXER GROUP to pay plaintiffs LUDMILA ERSHOV and PETER SCHWALBENBERG, individually and as trustees for the SCHWALBENBERG FAMILY REVOCABLE TRUST the sum of $55,000.00. Within 120 days of this date of Jan. 29, 2007 defendant JOHN B. WONG, individually and dba BOXER GROUP to pay plaintiffs LUDMILA ERSHOV and PETER SCHWALBENBERG, individually and as trustees for the SCHWALBENBERG FAMILY REVOCABLE TRUST the sum of $65,000.00. Defendant JOHN B. WONG, individually and dba BOXER GROUP to pay plaintiffs LUDMILA ERSHOV and PETER SCHWALBENBERG, individually and as trustees for the SCHWALBENBERG FAMILY REVOCABLE TRUST to pay amount of $120,000.00 in periodic payments in the sum of $2,500.00 each month commencing March 1, 2007 for 48 months. Obligation of $16,233.00 to Sierra Electric and $42,602.00 to Marelich Mechanical will be satisfied by Mr. Wong. Each side to bear own costs and attorney's fees. All parties agreed to the settlement on the record. JUDGE DONDERO; CLERK: V. MU; REPORTER: JO ANN BRYCE, #3321 (305)

Notice Of Motion Andmotion To Compel Discovery Responses From Defts John Wong (An Individual) And The Boxer Group;
Off Calendar Jan-03-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff Ludmila Ershov's Motion To Compel Discovery Responses From Defendants John Wong And The Boxer Group is moot as responses have been served. The court will not determine the adequacy of those responses. Counsel must meet and confer. The court grants relief from waiver of the right to object. Counsel are advised that there are a number of interrogatories and requests for documents which the court would not order further responses as they invade financial privacy without good cause. However, objections to the use of the term incident as ambiguous would not be sustained. Any documents withheld pursuant to a claim of privilege must be identified with specificity. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Default Prove-Up Hearing this day.
Dept. 218, VJ: Ollie Marie-Victoire, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: Rita Howell, Bailiff: A. Cruz.
James Sell, Esq. and Edward Seidel, Esq. for Plaintiffs: Ludmila Ershov and Peter Schwalbenberg, as trustees for the Schwalbenberg Family revocable Trust, all are present, were sworn and direct examined.
Garson Soe, Esq. is also present as a third party claimant, motion filed July 14, 2006.
After hearing the testimony and evidence presented, the Court rules in favor of the Plaintiffs and against the Defaulted Defendant: John B. Wong, ind. and dba The Boxer Group as follows:
Damages $359,716.25
Attorney Fees $35,409.50
Costs $1,429.50, for a Total of $396,555.25
